header.topic: How to Present and Deliver your PowerPoint

learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 10 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - final lesson of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit, concluding the 'About me' presentation golden thread for the term.
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils automated their presentation's slide timings and made final improvements against the full unit checklist.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: This is the final lesson of the Term 1-2 sequence; the next unit begins next term.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils know the different techniques used to deliver a presentation, understand and evaluate what makes a presentation good, and can present by delivering their completed 'About Me' PowerPoint to the class.
assessment_criteria: Each student will deliver their 'About Me' presentation to the class or a small group.
Each student will use at least two delivery techniques discussed in the lesson (e.g. eye contact, pace, not reading directly from the slide).
Each student will give at least one piece of specific, constructive feedback to a peer using the success criteria.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Whole-class or small-group delivery of pupils' completed 'About Me' PowerPoint, with structured peer feedback against agreed success criteria, concluding the unit.
expected_outcomes.cross_curricular_or_real_world_link: Presentation delivery skills (eye contact, pace, structure) are used across subjects wherever pupils present work aloud, and in life beyond school in any situation requiring public speaking.

key_vocabulary.definition: The way a presentation is spoken and performed to an audience; everyday meaning relates to transporting goods.
An agreed, specific set of statements describing what a good version of the task looks like, used to give and receive feedback.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Offer a lower-pressure small-group presenting option for pupils who need it, rather than whole-class delivery, while still meeting the assessment criteria.
accessibility: Small-group presenting option for anxious pupils
Presenter notes permitted rather than requiring fully memorised delivery

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Ask pupils to open their finished presentation and note, on mini-whiteboards, one thing they are proud of in it.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Recall and note one strength of their finished 'About Me' presentation.

check_for_understanding.question: What does it mean for a presentation to be 'self-running'?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed if most pupils correctly recall automation; otherwise briefly recap.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Introduce delivery techniques (eye contact, pace, projecting your voice, not reading directly from the slide, a clear introduction and conclusion) and agree success criteria for peer feedback as a class.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Discuss and help agree the success criteria that will be used for feedback.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Model delivering a short presentation using the agreed techniques, thinking aloud about pace and eye contact, then invite the class to give one piece of feedback against the success criteria.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch the model and give one piece of feedback against the success criteria.

check_for_understanding.question: Which success criterion did the model presentation meet most clearly?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Move to presenting regardless; use responses to check the success criteria are understood.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Organise presenting in small groups (or to the whole class, depending on time and confidence), circulating to observe delivery and prompt use of the agreed techniques.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Deliver their completed 'About Me' PowerPoint to their group or the class, using at least two agreed delivery techniques, and give structured peer feedback using the checklist.

check_for_understanding.question: Did you use eye contact and avoid reading directly from the slide?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils who read directly throughout are given one specific technique to focus on for their next presentation opportunity.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Bring the class together to reflect on the whole unit: what pupils found easiest, hardest, and most improved between their mind map and their final presentation.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Share one thing they improved between their mind map and their final presentation, and one delivery technique they want to keep working on.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: which delivery technique will you focus on next time you present?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: No pass threshold; responses inform which delivery techniques to revisit at the start of the next presenting opportunity.
